info.title: Privacy Automation - Incident Management
info.description: The Incident Management API allows you to efficiently manage and respond to incidents.

hostname.description: The OneTrust hostname such as app.onetrust.com, app-eu.onetrust.com, app-de.onetrust.com, app-uk.onetrust.com, app-apac.onetrust.com, trial.onetrust.com, or uat.onetrust.com.

tags.name: Incidents
tags.description: APIs used to create, update, search, and manage incidents, including linking incidents to inventories, retrieving details, and tracking their lifecycle.

post.description: Use this API to update the workflow stage for a specific incident by its unique identifier.

429.description:
Too Many Requests. 
For more information, see [API Rate Limits](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/rate-limits-overview).

schema.description: The number of seconds after which requests will be allowed again.
The unit of time for which the rate limit applies
The unique identifier for the rate-limiting event.
The number of requests made within the specified period.
The number of requests allowed within the specified period.

post.description: Use this API to update the workflow stage for a specific incident by its unique identifier and workflow type.

429.description:
Too Many Requests. 
For more information, see [API Rate Limits](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/rate-limits-overview).

schema.description: The number of seconds after which requests will be allowed again.
The unit of time for which the rate limit applies
The unique identifier for the rate-limiting event.
The number of requests made within the specified period.
The number of requests allowed within the specified period.

post.description: Use this API to create a new incident in the Incident Register by providing the necessary details in the request body. The response will return the unique identifier (UUID) of the newly created incident.

201.description: Incident created successfully. The response contains the UUID of the newly created incident.

429.description:
Too Many Requests. 
For more information, see [API Rate Limits](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/rate-limits-overview).

schema.description: The number of seconds after which requests will be allowed again.
The unit of time for which the rate limit applies
The unique identifier for the rate-limiting event.
The number of requests made within the specified period.
The number of requests allowed within the specified period.

post.description: Use this API to search for incidents by key terms and filters.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort criteria in the format: property,(asc|desc)

get.description: Use this API to retrieve a single incident by its unique identifier along with the linked assessments, status, and the associated attributes.

parameters.name: incidentId
parameters.description: ID of the incident. The value can be obtained using Create Incident API.

schema.description: Incident ID

429.description:
Too Many Requests. 
For more information, see [API Rate Limits](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/rate-limits-overview).

schema.description: The number of seconds after which requests will be allowed again.
The unit of time for which the rate limit applies
The unique identifier for the rate-limiting event.

The number of requests allowed within the specified period.

put.description: Use this API to update the details of a specific incident by providing the incident's unique ID in the URL path and the updated information in the request body. The response will return the updated incident information.

parameters.name: incidentId
parameters.description: ID of an incident. The value can be obtained using Create Incident API.

schema.description: Incident ID

post.description: Use this API to link an incident with a specific inventory type.

parameters.name: inventoryType
parameters.description: Type of inventory being linked. Allowed values: {assets, processing-activities, vendors, entities}

schema.description: Inventory Link Creation Request
List of inventory link associations

notifyIndividualDeadline.description: The recommended deadline by which an individual should be notified, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

notifyAuthorityDeadline.description: The recommended notification deadline for an authority, in ISO 8601 UTC format.

id.description: Unique identifier for the attribute option

valueKey.description: Translation key used for localizing the value

colorCode.description: Color code associated with the option. Used for score-based attributes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the attribute option

valueKey.description: Translation key used for localizing the value

colorCode.description: Color code associated with the option. Used for score-based attributes.

optionSelectionValue.description: Selection score value linked to the option. Used for score-based or numerical-based attributes.

displayLabel.description: Display name for the option, used for external attributes managed by other systems

disabled.description: Indicates whether this attribute option is currently disabled.

type.description: Indicates whether the assignee is an individual user or a group of users.

incidentTypeId.description: The unique identifier for the incident type.

incidentTypeName.description: The name of the incident type.

name.example: Joe sent an email to the wrong client

description.example:
An employee accessed a restricted HR file without proper authorization.
Access logs confirmed the breach, and the user’s permissions were immediately revoked.

rootCause.example:
The root cause was traced to a lack of validation in the email recipient auto-fill feature,
leading to sensitive data being sent to the wrong external recipient.

dateOccurred.description: The date and time when the incident occurred, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

dateClosed.description: The date and time when the incident was closed, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

dateDiscovered.description: The date and time when the incident was discovered, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

deadline.description: The date by which the incident investigation or notification must be completed, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

createdDT.description: The date and time when the incident was created, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

incidentStageBadgeColor.description: The badge color of the incident workflow stage.

schemaId.description: The unique identifier of the custom attribute schema associated with the incident.

updatedDT.description: The date and time when the incident was last updated, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

incidentAssigneeInformation.description: A list containing information about users or groups assigned to the incident

id.description: Unique identifier for the attribute option

valueKey.description: Translation key used for localizing the value

colorCode.description: Color code associated with the option. Used for score-based attributes.

optionSelectionValue.description: Selection score value linked to the option. Used for score-based or numerical-based attributes.

displayLabel.description: Display name for the option, used for external attributes managed by other systems

disabled.description: Indicates whether this attribute option is currently disabled.

assigneeEmail.description: Email of the incident assignee. This field is valid only when the assignee type is "USER".

type.description: Indicates whether the assignee is an individual user or a group of users.

countryCode.description: Three-digit country code of the jurisdiction.

stateProvinceCode.description: State Province Code. **Required if countryCode is 'USA' or 'CAN'**.

name.description:
Name of the incident.
Provide a descriptive name to the incident so that it can be quickly referenced in the incident register.
name.example: Joe sent an email to the wrong client

description.example:
An employee accessed a restricted HR file without proper authorization.
Access logs confirmed the breach, and the user’s permissions were immediately revoked.

incidentTypeName.description: The name of the type of incident that has occurred.

orgGroupId.description:
Incident Organization ID.
This can be retrieved from the [Get List of Organizations](/onetrust/reference/organizationtreestructureusingget) API.

dateOccurred.description: The date and time when the incident occurred, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

dateDiscovered.description: The date and time when the incident was discovered, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

deadline.description: The date by which the incident investigation or notification must be completed, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

rootCause.description: The root cause summary of the incident.
rootCause.example:
The root cause was traced to a lack of validation in the email recipient auto-fill feature,
leading to sensitive data being sent to the wrong external recipient.

notificationNeeded.description: Indicates whether a notification is required for this incident either to an authority or affected individuals.

attributeValues.description:
A map of custom attribute field names to the list of values associated with each attribute.
Each entry in the list can contain either an 'id' (for predefined options) or a 'value', depending on the attribute configuration.
If any attribute value is missing in the JSON array, the existing value for that attribute will be overridden, and the attribute will have empty values.

incidentAssigneeRequests.description: A list of incident assignee requests, where each request includes an assignee's ID, optional email, and type (USER or GROUP).

name.example: Joe sent an email to the wrong client

description.example:
An employee accessed a restricted HR file without proper authorization.
Access logs confirmed the breach, and the user’s permissions were immediately revoked.

This can be retrieved from the [Get List of Organizations](/onetrust/reference/organizationtreestructureusingget) API.

dateDiscovered.description: The date and time when the incident was discovered, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

deadline.description: The date by which the incident investigation or notification must be completed, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

rootCause.description: The root cause summary of the incident.
rootCause.example:
The root cause was traced to a lack of validation in the email recipient auto-fill feature,
leading to sensitive data being sent to the wrong external recipient.

notificationNeeded.description: Indicates whether a notification is required for this incident either to an authority or affected individuals.

attributeValues.description:
A map of custom attribute field names to the list of values associated with each attribute.
Each entry in the list can contain either an 'id' (for predefined options) or a 'value', depending on the attribute configuration.
If any attribute value is missing in the JSON array, the existing value for that attribute will be overridden, and the attribute will have empty values.

incidentAssigneeRequests.description: A list of incident assignee requests, where each request includes an assignee's ID, optional email, and type (USER or GROUP).

linkedInventories.description:
Map of inventory types to a single inventory link request.
Keys represent inventory types ('assets', 'processing-activities', 'vendors', 'entities'),
and the value contains either externalIds or inventoryIds.

autoAssessJurisdictions.description: If set to true, automatically assess jurisdictions after incident creation.

externalIds.description: Set of unique identifiers for the record from an external system

id.description: The unique identifier of the organization.
Unique identifier of the associated inventory

incidentTypeId.description: The unique identifier for the incident type.

incidentTypeName.description: The name of the incident type.

description.example:
An employee accessed a restricted HR file without proper authorization.
Access logs confirmed the breach, and the user’s permissions were immediately revoked.

dateOccurred.description: The date and time when the incident occurred, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

dateClosed.description: The date and time when the incident was closed, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

dateDiscovered.description: The date and time when the incident was discovered, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

deadline.description: The date by which the incident investigation or notification must be completed, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

totalAssessment.description: Count of Assessment Linked to this Incident

isExtended.description: Indicates whether the notification deadline has been extended.

isDeleted.description: Indicates whether the incident has been deleted.

createdDate.description: The date and time when the incident was created, represented as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

name.example: Joe sent an email to the wrong client

openAssessment.description: Count of Open Assessments Linked to this Incident

incidentStageBadgeColor.description: The badge color of the incident workflow stage.

filters.description: filters, which is a map of the field name and the value. Can filter on Incident Id, assignee Id, type Id, organization group Id, number, created By, is extended, is deleted

additionalProperties.description: filters, which is a map of the field name and the value. Can filter on Incident Id, assignee Id, type Id, organization group Id, number, created By, is extended, is deleted

fullText.description: full text search term. Can search on Incident name, description, type name, assignee name, organization group name, root cause, creator and stage name

nextStageName.description: Name of the next stage to navigate to

parameters.description: Additional parameters for workflow navigation customization

workflowExceptionApprovalRequiredFromAll.description: Is exception required from all approvers

approverUserId.description: Workflow Stage Approver User Id, User ID from Onetrust System

referenceApproverUserSourceId.description: Reference identifier for approver' source. ie, fieldName from attribute Manager

referenceApproverUserSourceOwner.description: Reference Source Owner. ie, schemaname from attribute manager

approverUserGroupId.description: Workflow Stage Approver User Group Id, User Group ID from Onetrust System

approverCategory.description: Field to indicate if the approver is for stage approval or exception
Approver Category for stage assignment: Stage/Exception

nameKey.description: Workflow Stage Name Key for translation purposes

descriptionKey.description: Workflow Stage Description Key for translation purposes

allowDeletion.description: Indicates if Workflow Stage can be deleted

badgeColor.description: Workflow Stage Badge Color for UI purposes

approvalStage.description: Indicates if the stage is an approval stage

exceptionStage.description: Indicates if the stage is an exception approval stage

autoAdvanceOnApproval.description: Can Workflow Stage Auto-Advance on Approval?

additionalFields.description: Additional fields associated with Workflow Stage

advanceStageActionEnabled.description: Does Workflow Stage has advance stage action enabled?

scopes.INCIDENT: Grants the user permission to view and manage the incident details.
scopes.INCIDENT_READ: Grants the user permission to read incident data, including retrieving incident details.
scopes.INCIDENT_CREATE: Grants the user permission to create incident.
